name: Module Boundary Check
description: Tier B (agentStop): After session completes, checks for cross-module boundary violations in modified files.

then.prompt:
## Package Territory Check (MANDATORY — evaluate FIRST)
Before performing any checks below, verify each file's path in your scan.

EXCLUDED ZONES (package infrastructure — NOT compliance subjects):
• .kiro/hooks/ — AI-GCE hook infrastructure
• .kiro/steering/ — AI-DWG/AI-GCE steering infrastructure
• .kiro/agents/ — AI-GCE agent infrastructure
• .governance/ — AI-GCE governance rules/agents/logs
• compliance-log/ — AI-GCE audit trail
• project-initiation/ — AI-PILC output
• architecture/ — AI-ADLC output
• management_framework/ — Shared governance spine
• docs/compliance-* — AI-GCE generated dashboards
• templates/ — AI-DWG planning templates

For each file in your scan: if its path starts with ANY of the above prefixes, SKIP it silently. Only check files outside excluded zones.

---

Check .compliance-state.json → currentPhase. This hook applies from Construction phase onward.

Review all files modified during this session that are within module source paths. For each:

1. CROSS-MODULE IMPORTS: Check if any file imports/references classes from another module directly. Cross-module communication MUST use events or APIs — not direct class references.
2. LAYER DIRECTION: Verify dependencies flow inward only (presentation → application → domain, infrastructure → domain). No outward dependencies.
3. SHARED KERNEL ONLY: If cross-module code is needed, it MUST come from the shared kernel — not from another module's internals.

If violations found, warn with rule MOD-02/MOD-03 and explain the correct pattern.
If all rules pass, confirm compliance silently.

## Compliance Logging
After completing all checks, append to `compliance-log/events/{today-date}.jsonl`:
{"timestamp": "{ISO-8601-UTC}", "type": "check", "id": "chk-{date}-{time}-{seq}", "hook": "module-boundary-check", "trigger": "agentStop", "ruleId": "{primary-rule-checked}", "ruleSeverity": "high", "result": "{pass|fail|warn}", "message": "{finding}"}
Log ONE event per rule checked.
